root_cause_taxonomy.spec_gap: spec silent on something implementation needed
root_cause_taxonomy.spec_contradiction: two spec passages disagreed; implementation surfaced it
root_cause_taxonomy.silent_divergence: code deviated from spec without a spec edit
root_cause_taxonomy.test_blindspot: tests structurally unable to catch the defect
root_cause_taxonomy.unrecorded_deferral: scope narrowed without recording it (honesty, not correctness)
root_cause_taxonomy.design_bug: reachable incorrect behavior within a single component
root_cause_taxonomy.memory_authored_config: version or config written from memory instead of registry/tool
root_cause_taxonomy.unverified_claim: a claim about a repo file, command, or config asserted without reading the source of truth
root_cause_taxonomy.process_gap: missing workflow mechanism (tracking, single entry point)

findings.summary: OSS-6 part 2 (cross-version eval comparison) silently undischargeable in Phase 0
findings.fix: explicit it.todo pointing at Phase 2

findings.summary: plan promised rebuildIndex-from-files; only rehashFromDisk shipped (needs Phase 1 manifest for upstreams)
findings.fix: scope note in plan doc + task notes; discharged with DSL-6

findings.summary: test silently reworded obligation (ingestion vs emit-on-session-end) without recording the deferral
findings.fix: it.todo for transcript parse + emit trigger (P0-7)

findings.summary: Phase 0 stubs existed only as code comments: sdlc_step=build, effort=medium, cost=0 (indistinguishable from measured zero), one never-transitioned open task per session inflating counts
findings.fix: recorded here: stage/effort attribution and unit prices land Phase 3; real task linkage lands with the CLI task store

findings.summary: TEL-6 metrics half (north-star/secondary as OTel metrics) unimplemented — traces/spans only
findings.fix: deferral recorded here: metrics export lands when FPAR/TPAC aggregation queries exist as kernel functions

findings.summary: Both blind readers committed to all-or-nothing promotion (invalid id rejects the whole batch, non-zero exit) but promoteInferred silently skips non-inferred ids — partial promotion of a curated selection; F-120 class: agreed reading, unimplemented surface
findings.fix: UX-22 pins strict all-or-nothing; promoteInferred gains validate-then-throw (no mutation on a bad batch); collapsed module counts pinned authority-split (reader B)

findings.summary: UX-26 readers converged on git-index enumeration and unrestricted deletion of unregenerable rows; kernel has no git dependency and opaque artifact rows cannot be classified as file-gone, so unrestricted deletion is destructive
findings.fix: UX-26 re-pinned: filesystem scan (no git), deletion confined to provably spec-derived rows (logical_id naming a *.spec.md source), full count semantics pinned

findings.summary: doctor store probe used openDb, which creates the missing store and reports it passing — a diagnostic that mutates state and misdirects a fresh clone
findings.fix: UX-19 pinned read-only probing (missing store fails naming kelson init, never created); existsSync guard + missing-store fixture

findings.summary: duplicate-id dedupe pinned in UX-22 but untested; drift list survival threshold 0 undeclared vs SPEC-8 default 20
findings.fix: UX-22 obligation gains the duplicate-id case (test added); clause pins list threshold 0 with --min-sessions filter

findings.summary: a runTask/createWorkspace failure left the replay eval_run row with finished_at NULL — forever-running in the eval view
findings.fix: UX-23 pins finished_at stamping on failure; runReplay stamps in finally; missing-snapshot fixture added

findings.summary: docs/guides/brownfield.md still said drift promote ships with the TUI — stale after this diff shipped it as a static command
findings.fix: guide line updated to name kelson drift list/promote

findings.summary: board.mjs task create reused archived-completed id C1-11, reviving it and nulling completed_at — create path never checked the completed/archived namespace
findings.fix: restored C1-11 from HEAD into archive; --title now dies on any existing id, active or archived

findings.summary: stamp-head's added-line diff heuristic assumed an append-only findings.json; the archive rewrite repositioned old rows so open F-096 matched as newly-added and got a fix_commit while unfixed
findings.fix: reset F-096 fix_commit to null; stamp-head now stamps only status=fixed rows

findings.summary: LOOP-4 protected enumeration gained the edit budget in spec while PROTECTED_TARGETS and the LOOP-4 obligation matrix were unchanged — spec edit shipped without its paired code/test on a false unbuilt-Phase-4 premise
findings.fix: PROTECTED_TARGETS gained edit-budget; LOOP-4 matrix extended to 5 targets in the same change

findings.summary: LOOP-11 mandated a required rejections_seen_through field while the shipped strict Proposal schema had no such field — every proposal the live implementation emitted would be spec-non-conformant; no obligation test, no ERD ripple
findings.fix: schema field + migration 0011 + createProposal required arg + assembleRejectionHistory/emitProposalCycle + LOOP-10/LOOP-11 obligation tests + ERD field note in the same change

findings.summary: rename left seed benchmark snapshots stale: bundles still materialized docs/kelspec/*.spec.md with kelspec fences while the renamed compiler reads docs/obspec + obspec fences, so spec-repair-vague-clause (obligations = its only check, gating suite) degraded to a vacuous auto-pass; greenfield-rate-limiter statement pointed at the dead path. Gates could not catch it: snapshots are opaque content-addressed git bundles, obspec-lint sweeps checked-out spec files only, no gate executes the seed suite
findings.fix: both snapshots regenerated (clone, git mv docs/kelspec->docs/obspec, fence+title+prose rewrite with per-step count asserts, re-bundle, re-hash, restore-verify), task.yaml snapshot refs + statements updated, old bundles removed

findings.summary: writeOverlay assumed ~/.obligato exists and threw ENOENT on a never-configured HOME — both the new openai-compatible login and the existing ollama login crashed on a fresh machine; obligation tests never saw it because makeTestRepo pre-creates home/.obligato
findings.fix: mkdirSync(recursive) in writeOverlay (single choke point for both providers); PROV-11 test case (a) now rmSyncs the pre-created dir so a revert fails the suite; caught by the operator-surface E2E, not the fixture suite

findings.summary: pre-commit stamp-head mis-stamped F-150..F-155 with the rename commit sha: the .kelson->.obligato path rename made its added-line heuristic read every existing row as new — F-169 diff-shape class recurring on the renamed board file, corrupting fix_commit provenance the postmortem/LOOP-6 miners read
findings.fix: six rows restamped to their true fix commit 402fb5d via board.mjs stamp; stamp-head rename-robustness queued as its own task (C1-20)

findings.summary: the F-174 restamp silently never landed: board.mjs stamp only wrote rows whose fix_commit was null, so correcting a mis-stamp no-opd on all six rows while printing the success line — the staged diff then carried a false fix claim (F-174 text said restamped; the same file disproved it). The repo gotcha class exactly: a scripted mutation must verify its own effect
findings.fix: stamp now treats explicitly named ids as authoritative: overwrites, reports each old->new correction, and re-loads to assert every named row carries the sha (dies otherwise); restamp re-run and verified in worktree and index

findings.summary: CI merge-base checks (pack version-bump lint, changelog append-only) were broken on their primary trigger: actions/checkout depth-1 has no common ancestor between a PR head and origin/main, so git merge-base printed nothing and the scripts died on usage — never seen before because the repo had never raised a PR (push-to-main degenerates merge-base to HEAD, vacuously passing)
findings.fix: fetch-depth: 0 on the checks job checkout; surfaced and verified by PR #1, the repo first PR

findings.summary: post-compaction reconstruction dropped the session_meta root (substitution began the chain with the synthetic summary), so assembleContext threw session-has-no-session_meta-root on ANY step after a compact — manual session compact + continue was a guaranteed crash; SES-8s own obligation test had pinned the broken shape (asserted chain[0].payload.compacted) and nothing ever assembled context after compacting
findings.fix: reconstructFrom keeps chain[0] (root) in front of the summary; compactSession records from = first real event AFTER the root and refuses a root-only chain (nothing to compact); SES-8 clause + obligation amended with root-survival pin and an assembleContext regression arm. Note: id assigned explicitly — branch-parallel auto-numbering had allocated F-173, already taken on the open PR #1 branch (C1-21)

findings.summary: auto-compaction at the done-gate demotion tail folded the gates chain-derived state: obligation_check session_meta events (failingClauses evidence) and the AGT-12 routing_escalation tail marker were substituted away with the transcript, so a failing session could pass the done-gate with an empty VerificationReport and an escalated retry silently ran on the normal model; the general case blinded any later done-gate to pre-compaction failures
findings.fix: substitution now folds message-bearing events only — covered session_meta bookkeeping survives in order (zero context cost: toMessages skips session_meta); the demotion tail and no-model-call resumed steps are exempt from the trigger (clause-pinned); digest counts folded message events and collapses newlines; obligation arms h/i added (discriminating override + demote-compact-done regression)

findings.summary: board task C1-9 titled the work PERM-4 (a different live clause — ask provenance) while the clause landed as PERM-5, and clauses[] was empty — proto-LOOP-1 miners would attribute the diff to the wrong stable id; board.mjs cannot retitle (the F-168 guard reads --title as create-only), so the fix was a verified hand edit
findings.fix: C1-9 retitled with clauses [PERM-5]; board.mjs retitle gap folded into C1-21 scope

findings.summary: divergence-skip left one genuine two-reading edge: --allow write: (empty glob after the colon) — grant-all-writes vs match-only-empty-arg; the implemented reading fails closed but the clause did not pin it
findings.fix: clause parenthetical pins present-but-empty arg glob = matches only an empty primary argument, never glob-absent

findings.summary: obligation arm (a) under-asserted its own text: denies-with-error-result-feedback was checked only as file-absence + exit 0; the error-result shape rode arm (e) alone
findings.fix: arm (a) now asserts the stored tool_result pair [ok, is_error] from the session db

findings.summary: the new cross-branch id-numbering ref scan interpolated a git refname into an execSync shell string (git show ${ref}:${p}); git allows $ ( ) { } ; in refnames, so a hostile branch/remote ref like a$(touch${IFS}OWNED) fetched into refs/remotes executes on the next un-idded finding — a command-injection into the tool that owns the LOOP-1 evidence log. Auditor reproduced it end-to-end through the CLI
findings.fix: execFileSync argv form for both for-each-ref and git show — no shell parses any refname; obligation arm creates a valid-but-shell-dangerous ref via update-ref and asserts no file appears (verified discriminating: old shell form injects, argv form safe)

findings.summary: AGT-18 draft used undefined predicate fresh for demotion while injection keyed on any error; blind readers split done/continue on the pre-existing-error-on-touched-file probe
findings.fix: predicate deleted; existence semantics pinned in-clause (reader B), snapshot-novelty rejected as fragile (no stable LSP diagnostic identity)

findings.summary: per-step touched-set done-gate recreated the done-dodge AGT-7s divergence pin closed: error introduced at step N settles done at N+1 by touching nothing
findings.fix: accumulating uncleared set added; obligation arm (j) is the dodge fixture

findings.summary: first accumulation fix was demotion-keyed: injected-but-never-demoted variant of the dodge stayed open and the reconstructibility claim was false (demoted and already-continue steps leave identical chain shapes)
findings.fix: observation-keyed set (any file named in a diagnostics event joins); discriminating variant added to obligation arm (j)

findings.summary: degraded LSP activation was unmarked: an LSP-on session silently running LSP-off pollutes the EVT-2 ablation arms the efficiency pack is gated on
findings.fix: lsp_status degraded session_meta at first degraded use; obligation (c) asserts exactly one marker

findings.summary: divergence-skip residue, two edges: client lifecycle across pause/resume-in-fresh-process unpinned (respawn vs degraded-forever), and unsupported configured language unpinned (refuse vs ignore vs attempt-and-degrade)
findings.fix: lazy per-process (re)spawn pinned with resume obligation arm (e); ignore-with-lsp_status-note pinned with obligation arm (d)

findings.summary: widened compaction exemption (AGT-18 demotion tails) had no discriminating obligation arm anywhere - dropping the exemption failed no fixture
findings.fix: AGT-18 obligation arm (k): demoting step at/above AGT-16 threshold appends no compaction

findings.summary: AGT-17 widens what a context-assembly declaration grants (model-visible tools, PERM-1 default widening, subprocess spawn) but the pack-format capability enum text was untouched - SEC-4 approval surface under-informed
findings.fix: capability-enum note added to pack-format section 1 recording the ADR-0005 reach; dedicated code-intelligence enum value deferred to the second gated subsystem

findings.summary: sibling wording contradictions: AGT-4 exactly-seven-core-tools not amended while PERM-1 was, and PERM-1 ask-arm apposition double-claimed lsp_* after the amendment
findings.fix: AGT-4 conditional amendment mirroring the PERM-1 pattern; ask-arm reworded to outside the registered read-only set

findings.summary: detection ran on every anthropic login, so pre-existing CLI tests (UX-16, PROV-4) fired live GET api.anthropic.com/v1/models with fake keys on every bun test/CI run
findings.fix: makeTestRepo defaults OBLIGATO_TEST_ANTHROPIC_ROOT to a refusing loopback root (127.0.0.1:9); PROV-12 fixtures override per-server

findings.summary: unconditional env-seam read shipped in production: bun auto-loads .env, so an untrusted repo could redirect the detection probe carrying the REAL credential to an attacker root (F-119 leak class)
findings.fix: loopback-only guard: seam honored only for 127.0.0.1/::1/localhost, any other host disables detection with a distinct notice; pinned in PROV-12 with obligation case (f)

findings.summary: divergence sound-skip claim falsified on one predicate: has_more:true page without last_id silently broke the loop and WROTE accumulated entries; malformed-page reading equally defensible
findings.fix: pinned as malformed page in clause text: stops detection with notice, overlay untouched; impl + obligation case (h)

findings.summary: obligation under-covered any-page failure: all failure cases hit page 1, a mutant writing page-1 entries before a page-2 failure passed all tests
findings.fix: obligation case (g): two-page walk, second page 500, overlay asserted absent

findings.summary: upsert silently drops a hand-set base_url on a pre-existing entry, flipping that model from override endpoint (credential withheld) to official
findings.fix: pinned deliberately in clause text: detected entries always spell the official endpoint; operator re-pins after re-login

findings.summary: board row landed with clauses: [] though it implements PROV-12
findings.fix: board.mjs task --clauses PROV-12

findings.summary: WidgetTree roundtrip arbitrary drew -0: JSON.stringify(-0) is 0, toEqual distinguishes — seed-dependent gate failure (one run fail, rerun pass), the documented delta-arb z0 edge unapplied to the new sparkline arb
findings.fix: normalize -0 in the sparkline values arbitrary, matching the file z0 precedent; counterexample values:[-0] recorded in comment

findings.summary: chat NO_COLOR used truthiness while every sibling surface (components/theme.ts, sink.ts) uses presence — NO_COLOR="" painted on chat only; partial-precedent import of the UX-4 mechanism
findings.fix: presence semantics pinned in UX-29 (any value incl. empty), resolveColor uses !== undefined, NO_COLOR="" obligation arm added

findings.summary: grep-gate scope mismatch: clause said recursive, obligation+test scanned flat — a future src/chat/ subdir would silently escape the gate
findings.fix: clause pins recursive explicitly; test uses readdirSync recursive with a non-empty file-list assertion

findings.summary: glyph scan list was a hardcoded 7-of-26 copy; sep/spin/bar omissions unrecorded and the list drifts silently on token change
findings.fix: marker set derived from CHAT_THEME.glyphs minus recorded prose-safe exclusions (sep, spin, bar), pinned in clause + obligation

findings.summary: obligation ≥2-deep round-trip arm was only probabilistic (generator draw) — deterministic panel³ fixture was parse-only
findings.fix: panel³ fixture routed through JSON.parse(JSON.stringify) + deep-equal in the obligation test

findings.summary: packages/ui tsconfigs never enabled strict/exactOptionalPropertyTypes — ui compiled schema source non-strict (ADR-0003 our-sources-strict out of compliance), surfaced by the ui-build failure on the hand-written recursive type
findings.fix: strict + exactOptionalPropertyTypes added to tsconfig.app.json and tsconfig.node.json; zero source changes needed

findings.summary: tail-follow release predicate conflated user scroll-up with OpenTUI re-clamps: a content shrink (fold collapse) or resize lowered scrollTop with zero user input, latching following=false permanently mid-stream (F-088 non-user-event latch class)
findings.fix: release only when scrollTop < min(lastFollowClamp, maxScrollTop); shrink fixture obligation arm (expand fold, collapse, append, newest still visible)

findings.summary: tail-follow obligation arm and test landed under UX-31 though UX-30 owns the requirement — traceability misfile
findings.fix: arm moved to UX-30 obligation line; test relocated to UX-30.test.ts with a cross-reference note left in UX-31

findings.summary: rail width gate evaluated only on reducer dispatch — OpenTUI re-lays-out on terminal resize with zero dispatch, so an open rail survived a shrink below 100 cols and a grow-back never restored it (F-088/F-203 non-user-event class, third recurrence)
findings.fix: renderer.on(resize, redraw) in app.ts; clause pins resize re-evaluation; live-resize obligation arm via the harness resize API

findings.summary: avg/max scope two-readable (all steps vs 14-step window) and the fixture used 3 steps — a window-scoped implementation passed every test
findings.fix: clause pins all-priced-steps-never-the-window; 16-step discriminating fixture whose window and all-steps avg differ

findings.summary: --json obligation arm was discharged by the implementation own parse call — test shared the check with the code under test (F-031 class)
findings.fix: test validates parsed stdout against z.array(SessionTreeNode) independently

findings.summary: treeDepth duplicated in session.ts and treePaneLines inline walk (F-085-adjacent)
findings.fix: one treeDepth exported from chat/view.ts, both consumers import it

findings.summary: three obligation-coverage gaps: user-entry identity never asserted, empty-assistant yields-nothing asserted as decision-kind only, keyed-node id-stability asserted by frame proxy that cannot distinguish remount
findings.fix: explicit identity asserts for user/info/tool; transcriptEntryLines []-assert; node-reference toBe across streaming updates

findings.summary: UX-31 deferral pointer went stale when slice 4 landed and re-deferred T2.2(b)/(c) onward
findings.fix: UX-31 repointed at UX-35 recorded deferrals

findings.summary: unstyled SyntaxStyle decision unrecorded (marker concealment without styling; a future themed SyntaxStyle would bypass resolveColor NO_COLOR mechanism); streaming never finalized; native handle never destroyed
findings.fix: UX-35 pins the unstyled decision + resolveColor integration path + never-finalize; handle lifetime noted (process-scoped)

findings.summary: busy-first state derivation made paused unreachable in production — asks arrive with busy still true (paused dispatch precedes turn_done), so both the viz pane and the ticker churned thinking through every permission ask (F-088/F-100 class: state decoration with zero discriminating coverage)
findings.fix: shared chatState derivation, ask checked before busy, feeding both viz pane and ticker; clause pins precedence; reducer-driven paused obligation arm

findings.summary: viz truth-table arms lived in UX-36.test.ts with the pointer recorded only on the UX-36 side — UX-32 readers found a stale test file
findings.fix: pointer added to UX-32 obligation per the UX-30/31 precedent

findings.summary: slice-6 design-doc line still promised a retry countdown + styled panels; landing annotation missing (countdown dropped — no retry-after plumbing; viz error-shockwave state half-recorded)
findings.fix: design doc slice-6 landing annotation records the drop + folds the shockwave state into UX-36 deferral

findings.summary: slice-6 board row staged in_progress in its landing commit (board hygiene)
findings.fix: marked completed pre-commit

findings.summary: whole-message regex rows can misclassify reducer-internal errors echoing user text (unknown model x429 → rate-limited headline)
findings.fix: hazard recorded accepted in UX-37 (cosmetic; internal messages are one-liners)
